subjects.name: English Language (Prelims)
subjects.analysis: RC dominates. Sentence rearrangement and error detection are high-scoring areas.

subjects.name: Quantitative Aptitude (Prelims)
subjects.analysis: Arithmetic heavy. Quadratic equations and Data Interpretation are mandatory for clearing cutoffs.

subjects.name: Reasoning Ability (Prelims)
subjects.analysis: Puzzle heavy. Efficiency in circular and linear seating is essential for the 20-minute sectional limit.

subjects.name: Reasoning & Computer Aptitude (Mains)
subjects.analysis: High level puzzles and critical reasoning dominate. Computer aptitude focuses on logic-based binary/flowcharts.

subjects.name: Data Analysis & Interpretation (Mains)
subjects.analysis: Advanced DI heavy. Questions are mostly arithmetic-based caselets and probability-based DI.

subjects.name: General Awareness (Mains)
subjects.analysis: Deep focus on Banking, Financial Awareness, and Economy. Merit is decided here.

subjects.name: Descriptive English (Mains)
subjects.analysis: Evaluation of typing speed and formal writing skills. Compulsory for merit.

mentorship_data.title: Prelims Strategy
mentorship_data.content: Focus on high-weightage topics like Puzzles and DI first. Sectional timing (20 mins) means you must pick the right questions to attempt.

mentorship_data.title: Mains Strategy
mentorship_data.content: GA + Data Analysis focus is mandatory. General Awareness is the biggest score booster; start preparing 6 months in advance.

mentorship_data.title: Phase III Strategy
mentorship_data.content: Banking awareness and current economic issues are vital for the Interview. Practice mock GEs for group dynamics.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there an interview in SBI PO?
frequently_asked_questions.a: Yes, Phase III includes a Psychometric Test, Group Exercise, and Personal Interview.
Yes, it is conducted online immediately after the objective Mains and is mandatory for merit calculation.
There is a 1/4th (0.25) negative marking for every wrong answer in Phase I and Phase II.

frequently_asked_questions.q: How is the final merit list calculated?
frequently_asked_questions.a: The final merit is based on marks obtained in Mains (Phase II) and Phase III. Prelims marks are not added.
It is a personality assessment test used to profile candidates before the interview; it has no specific marks but is reviewed by the panel.
